ASMR，全稱 Autonomous sensory meridian response，意為自發性知覺高潮反應，是一種對於視覺、聽覺、觸覺、嗅覺等其他知覺，顱內、頭皮、後背以及四肢等周邊部位受到刺激而產生愉悅反應的感知現象。該現象的本質和分類尚存在很大爭議，相關證據眾說紛紜，但目前仍然缺少科學解釋或研究數據來支撐相關的說法。

Autonomous sensory meridian response (ASMR) is a tingly experience characterized by a static-like or tingling sensation on the skin that typically begins on the scalp and moves down the back of the neck and upper spine, precipitating relaxation. Though it is usually a tingly feeling, it is also sometimes a feeling of sleepiness. How this feels might be like walking outside on a cold night and your hair stands on end from the cold. It has been compared with auditory-tactile synesthesia. Autonomous sensory meridian response (ASMR) signifies the subjective experience of 'low-grade euphoria' characterized by 'a combination of positive feelings, relaxation, and a distinct static-like tingling sensation on the skin'. It is most commonly triggered by specific acoustic, visual and digital media stimuli, and less commonly by intentional attentional control.
